differences.title: 1. TEMPLATING
differences.leftDesc: Uses familiar HTML syntax for templates, easy to learn for beginners.
differences.rightDesc: Combines UI and logic in JavaScript, offers full programming power.

differences.title: 2. STATE MANAGEMENT
differences.leftDesc: Vue's reactivity system is core, simple for local state.
differences.rightDesc: React uses Context API & Hooks for state sharing.

differences.title: 3. LEARNING CURVE
differences.leftDesc: Easier to pick up due to HTML templates and clear structure.
differences.rightDesc: JSX and functional concepts can take more time to master.

differences.title: 4. ECOSYSTEM SIZE
differences.leftDesc: Strong community and official libraries like Vuex, Vue Router.
differences.rightDesc: Largest community, countless libraries and tools available.

differences.title: 5. FLEXIBILITY
differences.leftDesc: Can be adopted incrementally in existing projects.
differences.rightDesc: React guides more towards specific patterns and practices.

differences.title: 6. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Virtual DOM and efficient diffing algorithm.
differences.rightDesc: Virtual DOM, requires careful optimization for best results.

differences.title: 7. TOOLING
differences.leftDesc: Excellent browser extensions for debugging and profiling.
differences.rightDesc: Rich development tools and linters available from community.
